rationale: The citing paper uses Cavanaugh (2014) in a specific theoretical role, not just as a general background citation. In the theory section, it draws on Cavanaugh to propose a concrete alternative account: lonely consumers may feel less deserving and avoid indulgence, which could make lower-priced used products appealing for frugality reasons. The authors then design analyses to assess this alternative and report no supporting evidence, so the citation materially informs framing and interpretation even though it is not the focal mechanism.
relation_description: The citing paper leverages Cavanaugh (2014) to articulate a competing deservingness/frugality explanation for loneliness effects, then empirically rules that account out for its used-product preference findings. Thus, it uses the cited work as a substantive contrastive benchmark in mechanism testing.
